title: orchis mascula subsp. hispanica
intro: Orchis mascula subsp. hispanica, also known as the Spanish orchid or wild orchid, is a subspecies of the Orchis mascula species, a type of flowering plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Orchis mascula subsp. hispanica include its ability to improve respiratory function, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve congestion and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in tea infusions or made into capsules, tinctures, and syrups to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
